channel.title: SOC 2 on RaihnForge
channel.description: Recent content in SOC 2 on RaihnForge

item.title: ITAM Internship A Reflection
item.description:
1. Learning Objectives

My first learning objective was to gain experience as an Agile project-management scrum master for the development team. Though I assist in prioritization and help unblock the development team throughout sprints, our team does not ascribe to strict scrum implementation. I also prefer to avoid the scrum points system, as it creates a distraction and an alluring incentive for team members to prioritize the gamified system over the actual applied outcomes. My objective was modified to increase confidence and organization during developer daily standups and bi-weekly meetings. I incorporated weekly one-on-one meetings with individual developers in order to assess and assist with progress and prioritization.

item.title: The Buyer's Experience
item.description:
A note on naming. The original paper named the specific vendor and salesperson. For public posting, both have been generalized: “the vendor” and “the sales representative.” The analysis is preserved verbatim. The intent was always portfolio reflection on the frameworks, not commentary on any individual professional.

TO: Robert Lupton FROM: Joshua Keyes DATE: June 2, 2023 SUBJECT: The Buyer’s Experience, Final

Product Information

One of my responsibilities as project manager for Kelvin Education, Inc. is assisting in the vetting of potential vendors that are involved in our SOC 2 certification project. I was responsible for leading the search for the needed auditing firm that would be a good fit for our organization’s specialization and processes. We have already been utilizing a compliance monitoring platform as a consulting and monitoring vendor for certification readiness. When it came time for audit selection, that platform recommended several firms to us, and the vendor described below was one of them. I scheduled an appointment and met with the vendor as part of our procurement process on Friday, November 4, at ten in the morning. The sales representative was the point of contact. The meeting was conducted virtually through Zoom (Keyes, 2022).
